question: Are several (more than two) farmworkers added to the wheat field?
Do the added farmworkers appear to be wearing wide-brimmed straw hats?
Are the farmworkers depicted in a bent-over posture consistent with binding sheaves?
Are wheat sheaves (bundles) visible near the workers, indicating the action of binding?
Is the scale of the added farmworkers consistent with the high-altitude aerial perspective (appearing appropriately small)?
Do the farmworkers show varied poses and realistic spacing (avoiding identical copy-paste patterns)?
Are exactly two white herons added to the sky?
Are the herons depicted in flight with wings spread, clearly recognizable as birds?
Do the herons blend naturally into the sky with appropriate lighting and clean edges?
Are the existing farm buildings (barns and farmhouse) completely unchanged?
Are the surrounding trees and distant mountains preserved without alteration?
Is the texture and track pattern of the wheat field preserved in areas not occupied by the new workers?
Is the sky area preserved (clouds and gradient) aside from the added herons?
Are the added farmworkers free from visual artifacts, distortions, or unnatural blurring?
Are the added herons free from halos, jagged edges, or anatomical distortions?
